author: @plutokrazo
text:
I modified this to this with Pi 

It sounds like you've described a fascinating setup for a simple electromagnetic generator. This device uses the mechanical vibrations of a slinky to produce electricity through electromagnetic induction. Let's break down the components and how they work together:
Components
Tube: The tube serves as a housing for the slinky and provides a pathway for its movement. It also helps guide the diaphragm's motion.
Slinky: The slinky acts as a spring that can vibrate back and forth. When stretched and released, it oscillates, transferring kinetic energy to the diaphragm.
Rubber Diaphragm: The diaphragm is attached to one end of the slinky. It moves up and down in response to the slinky's vibrations.
Magnets: These are attached to the diaphragm. As the diaphragm moves, the magnets move with it.
Copper Coil: The coil is placed around the area where the magnets move. It is stationary and does not touch the magnets.
How It Works
Vibration Initiation: The slinky is stretched and then released, causing it to vibrate. These vibrations are transferred to the diaphragm.
Magnetic Motion: As the diaphragm moves up and down, the magnets attached to it move through the coil of copper wire.
Electromagnetic Induction: The movement of magnets through the coil creates a changing magnetic field. According to Faraday's Law of Electromagnetic Induction, this changing magnetic field induces an electric current in the coil.
Electricity Generation: The induced current in the coil can be harnessed as electricity. This setup generates electricity passively as long as the slinky continues to vibrate.
Applications and Considerations
Energy Source: The energy source for this setup is the initial kinetic energy given to the slinky. To maintain continuous electricity generation, an external force or mechanism may be needed to keep the slinky vibrating.
Efficiency: The efficiency of this generator depends on factors such as the strength of the magnets, the number of turns in the coil, and the frequency of the diaphragm's movement.
Practical Use: While this setup can demonstrate the principles of electromagnetic induction, scaling it up for practical electricity generation would require careful design and optimization.
This setup is a creative way to explore the principles of physics and electricity generation. If you're building this as a project, ensure that all components are securely attached and that the magnets and coil are appropriately aligned for maximum efficiency.

author: @zeph0shade
text: This "vibration energy" harvesting sounds like piezoelectrics to me. It's already done and it's already cheap, but to get any meaningful power you have to use it somewhere you know is going to be subjected to a lot of impacts or "vibrations", essentially repeated compression and decompression, because even then it doesn't generate a huge amount. You might power a couple of light bulbs with piezoelectrics in the floor of a big room with lots of foot traffic, but I don't think a wind generator setup  *when there is no wind blowing* would ever provide enough power for anyone to notice unless you kept tapping on it like you did here. Or if there's an earthquake, perhaps. In the end it's a neat idea but I think someone's probably already thought of it before and decided the amount of energy you'd generate isn't worth further increasing the complexity of wind turbines. Better to just keep them as two separate types of power generation used in places where either one or the other makes more sense.

author: @jonc4271
text:
Wish you would get what you are talking about right in your description. As dose it t generate WIND ??? As you say it is a wind generate. But it is an ELECTRICAL generator POWERED by the wind. 
Just as a water wheel dose NOT generate water, it transforms the energy from the water into some other form of power. This could be physical power to turn a stone to grind up to make flour, or if you hook it up to a electrical generator, then it will make electricity. 
So what you are describing is an ELECTRICAL wind power generation system. 

SORRY for sounding pedantic, but I try to use the right words to explain and describe things. 

I can give you a good example of this. As many years ago I worked for what was then British Rail. A guy asked me when the Newcastle train was due. I asked him did he mean the train going to Newcastle or the train coming from Newcastle ??? He told me that it was obviously the train coming from Newcastle. I told him that Ben it was due, but told him next time he was to ask anyone, to ask them about the train FROM or the train TO. 
As I could have quite easily told him about the train going to Newcastle, and he would have came at the wrong time and gotten mad by MY wrong information.

author: @thomasloper1205
text:
It's a different form of an audio speaker. A speaker takes electricty and vibrates the diaphram to produce sound waves. This takes sound waves or other vibrations and uses it to move the diaphram and thus generate electricity. No doubt it works but I don't think you could generate much electricity with it. The long tube increases the amount of vibration it can capture and transfer to a very small diaphram. So it's more like a sound vibration telescope. 
In theory you could capture the vibration of every wall of every building and turn that back into electricity. But the energy in each of the vibrations it captures can't be that much. Put it next to a jet engine and capture that sound and you might generate some power. But the jet engine is using energy to make the vibrations.

text:
Hi Robert :  I’m gob smacked, I used to be an avid sci-fi reader since childhood, since then I’ve transferred to Fantasy. The reason I tell you this is because I can remember vividly from a book I read positing, like you said, the idea of harvesting power from the wind.  The methodology was not described so I remember thinking of this off and on for years how this could possibly be done. author: @uniqdzign2
text: Now I am not an inventor, but I am a musician. How about harnessing the energy from an Aeolian Harp? An instrument that is vibrated by air passing through a wide narrow slit and vibrating several strings. Popular I believe in Victorian times, when designed to sit in an open sash window, held down by the weight of the window above. Used to help children sleep in hot summer nights, when the wind blew gently through the device. The Strings were tightened over a box, (rather like a zither), which amplified the sound. Amplified sound equals more vibrations. To avoid noise pollution on larger boxes, the tuning could be kept to lower frequencies. A normal guitar string which is not tuned to its expected tension, vibrates considerably more, and also therefore gives much less noise. As for a pole mounted version, exciting the strings from external forces other than wind, it would still have a similar effect of 'moving a loose string.'

author: @davemould4638
text: Hmmmm.  I note the power output was not measured.  Current output means nothing without knowing the voltage across the output at that current flow (i.e. voltage and current must be measured at the same time).  As the output is across the extremely low resistance of a digital current meter (almost a short), the voltage (and hence power) will be close to zero.  Certainly in the low mV range at best, so I'd be surprised if it's giving as much as 5mW of power, meaning you'd need hundreds of the devices for just one watt of peak power, and I doubt that you'd get more than a ten-fold increase even with optimisation, so it's not really practical as a power generator.

For the past thirty odd years I've been insisting that the way to cleaner, cheaper and safer energy distribution is to go low-voltage domestic, wherever possible. Brazed to the inner vessel are an array of radial heat-pipes that conducts some of the inner vessel's heat - through the vacuum space - to the outer vessel, via ceramic 'ports' where the heatpipes are brazed to radial heatsinks surrounding the outer vessel.  The whole tank is further encased in a box of mica insulation chips (loft insulation).  Attached to each  heat sink are the 'hot' ends of an array of thermocouples, with the 'cold' ends attached to another set of heatsinks located on the inner surface the encasing box; an extra coolant circuit could be employed.  The current generated by the 'radial thermopile' should be adequate enough to run the the ancilliaries of the whole system - pumps - valves - uProcessors, controllers etc...    Combine this with good house insulation and solar-kinetic electrical generation - PV - wind - your 'energy harvester' and we get a 'net zero' lifestyle - more or less...

The Radio Isotope Prolonged-Powered Life Equipment - RIPPLE generator uses a similar array of thermocouples on heatsinks, that surround a casing containing a plutonium pellet the releases heat as it decays.  In the relatively cold vacuum of space, thermocouples are ideal, giving the thermal gradient required to generate enough wattage to run the vehicle and its sensors.

author: @chrisengland5523
text:
YouTube is full of geniuses that amazingly have somehow single-handedly solved all the energy problems that regular scientists have struggled with for decades. But less amazingly, none of their inventions seem to make it to the market. I wonder why. 

In this case he (allegedly) gets a few hundred milliamps by tapping the pole. But at what voltage? Power = volts x amps and if the voltage is near zero, then so is the power. Since the milliammeter is directly across his "generator", we can assume that the voltage is negligible - and therefore so is the power. In any case, the energy transferred to the device by flicking it with is fingers is minute and the output power can't be greater than the input power. So I don't think we're going to see this contraption producing any useful energy anytime soon.

author: @geoffthomas8528
text:
I find this verry interesting ..John.  but I think you will find a similar principle method that was used in the early reverb units. This comprised of a lengthy spring connected in the middle of its length and the other two ends were connected to a small magnet that was suspended in the center of a small coil. The coil was part of an audio amplifier circuit that would feed an analogue signal through it causing it to vibrate hence, increasing its potential that would be managed by i think a differential amplifier circuit and the subsequent outputs fed to the main amp. These reverb units are also used the hammond organs to enhance the sound. But I have to say that reverse engineering into a generator is ingenious. 
I enjoy watching you videos with a great deal of interest and I admire your enthusiasm and Comarardary. Best wishes, Geoff.

author: @georgekurgansky
text: 200ma is very very small. A solar cell is far, far more space efficient you'd see 1 - 12A in that much space on any day. At leat 5x more and 60x the power at best. Plus generally structures avoid resonance. It's kind of a bad microphone, it would serve better as an audio toy or a sensor. It would be hard to scale as well just because vibrations are so destructive and undesirable. Lamp post foundations would crack faster.  It would be good if it had a vibration dampening effect or incorporated in vibration dampening. But anyway, vibration is kind of waste energy, it's barely a fraction of what causes vibration. The source of vibration will give up more energy for the materials used. May be a piezoelectric device could suck up more energy by resonating at extremely high levels. Thing needs to be seriously moving though to compete, it's got to be hitting as hard as the wind. Ducted wind reed or something. It'd be noisier than a wind farm.

Came up with some improvements (about halfway through video):

    1. The tube uses air entering through the orifice on the side to shake a diaphragm that generates electricity, and it can vibrate up and down along the length axis of the tube. Using air, it would be more effective and powerful to use a focusing horn to make the pressure vibrations larger.

    2. Using different diameters, weights, and elasticity of diaphragms you can absorb a certain frequency of oscillation that is left out by the standard design, including using this to absorb bridge vibrations like a dampener or energy recovery from traffic.

    3. This is not much different to how an unpowered microphone works.

    4. Using materials that have a higher stiffness, or young's modulus, allows energy to transfer into the diaphragm more efficiencly without various substructures flexing, as well as reducing the overall weight.

    5. There is not much benefit to using artificial plastic muscles versus a standard spring or pneumatic, unless the flexural energy lost to heat is lower than other materials and methods.*

Came up with some improvements (about halfway through video):

    1. The tube uses air entering through the orifice on the side to shake a diaphragm that generates electricity, and it can vibrate up and down along the length axis of the tube. Using air, it would be more effective and powerful to use a focusing horn to make the pressure vibrations larger.

    2. Using different diameters, weights, and elasticity of diaphragms you can absorb a certain frequency of oscillation that is left out by the standard design, including using this to absorb bridge vibrations like a dampener or energy recovery from traffic.

    3. This is not much different to how an unpowered microphone works.

    4. Using materials that have a higher stiffness, or young's modulus, allows energy to transfer into the diaphragm more efficiencly without various substructures flexing, as well as reducing the overall weight.

    5. There is not much benefit to using artificial plastic muscles versus a standard spring or pneumatic, unless the flexural energy lost to heat is lower than other materials and methods.*
Warie Porbeni
Founder and president of Radio Zeater Club radiozeaterclub@gmail.com*

author: @MasterFeiFongWong
text:
This may be off topic, but someone might find it interesting. 
Over unity electric generator. By AMA. 
 Segment 1: 1 motor with bar connected to it standing vertically. Circular disc connected to bar that has magnets embedded in it surrounded in high permeability material to focus magnets outward pushing force in an upwards direction from top of disc. 
 Segment 2: Hollowed out cylindrical Bar of material horizontally positioned. 
 Flat hollowed out ring shaped material with openings around its outer top flat edge.
 Magnets surrounded in high permeability material embedded in these openings.
Magnets positioned in openings around ring are in at least 1 of 2 configurations either all N magnetic field pushing out or N,S,N,S all around it. Just depends on whether your going for Piston structure or spinning structure explained below & whether you want it to be DC or AC current. 
Place these all along the cylindrical bar connected to it solidly, except at far ends of bar. 
At both ends of bar place ring of material that can be magnetically repelled in specific locations. (magnetically repelled in specific location is in reference to outside of ring area that faces away from center of bar)(Other side of same ring facing in towards center of bar is capable of being magnetically repelled all around) Ring must be connected solidly to bar. points of repulsion on these rings is off set from each other on each end of bar. 
3 Hollow ring structures with small square bars protruding from each. Ring structure has all magnetic repulsion on inside of ring shape & can rap around the main bar so that the bar wont be causing friction on things as it moves back and forth or spins. 1st positioned around far left of bar, 2nd position at center of bar (be sure to leave enough room for magnetic structures to be able to move back and forth without hitting it) & 3rd positioned on far right of bar. 
Tube structure is constructed in two parts that fit together on top of each other length wise over main structure. 
Tube structure has 3 square openings for the bars that the Ring structures that are connected around the main bar keeping it from causing friction on structure, to slide into. 
This tube structure is designed with material that does not block magnetic fields. Perhaps some type of transparent material glass or other. 
 At both ends of the tube structure there is a built in circular groove that will house a round disc. The ceiling/floor/left side/& right side of groove has openings for small magnets to be  embedded in it, so that the pushing force from magnets is pushing in towards the edge of disc that goes in this spot. There is also 4 openings located on the top, bottom, left & right positions of the open groove. Small magnets are placed in these spots so when round disc is in position these magnetic fields will hit sideways keeping it from generating friction against structure. 
Far Ends of bar has Cap structure that screws over tube halves holding them together. Cap structure also has 4 points on inside top, bottom, left & right that have openings. These openings have small embedded magnets in them that exert there pushing force outwards (sideways) so it will keep disc in position, generating no friction against structure. Cap is made of material like tube so it does not block magnetic fields. (Note: These magnets in groove and cap structure are only needed for piston design. If Disc is directly connected to main bar so as it spins the bar spins, magnets are not needed here).
 The disc has ring around it (ring of material goes around outer edge and folds over front side & backside a small distance) that is repelled by magnetic fields so the 12 small magnets will keep it lined up but it will be able to spin without causing friction on structure. The disc has openings on it's flat side that face inside of tube and magnets surrounded in high permeability material are embedded in this disc. 
 The disc has small bar that is connected to it's center that goes all the way to the other end of  bar structure through the center of the hollowed out bar and connects to disc on other end. Disc on other end is set up the same but magnets embedded in it are off set in comparison. As first disc spins the magnetic fields will push against specific spot on disc connected directly to bar, pushing bar in other direction, once bar is fully pushed over, further spinning of disc will then align the other sides magnet to push it back.
 Tube structure also has half moon shaped protrusion on inside and on both halves so when tube is connected they line up to make a full ring shape on inside of tube, these half moon shaped protrusions have at least 1 small round opening on it's side in middle of curve that faces away from center of bar. The opening has a small magnet embedded in it so magnetic force is repelling out sideways away from center of bar. The protruding ring is positioned to line up slightly further in towards center of bar then the ring of magnetically repelled material connected directly to bar. This way when bar moves right or left it is magnetically stopped after a short distance. 
Copper coil setup: Copper coils are wound in at least 1 of 2 ways. Either coiled in circles next to each other (Like if you wound coils around a cylindrical bar) or laid out in a row as if wire was on flat table going up then bent to go down & back up until it formed a flat rectangle of wire bending up and down and then the entire structure folded around tube. Copper windings are then lined up perpendicular to magnetic fields on outside of tube. Ends of copper coils are connected into separate construct that will allow electrical current to flow somewhere else. 
Circular construct is built like a stand that goes around the outside of motor segment. Circular construct has flat ring of high permeability (magnetic field shielding) material that has small openings that will allow magnetic fields through specific locations. Top of circular construct has groove to allow the horizontal bar to balance on. Circular construct can also be placed on other end of bar so it is balanced. The motor segment is positioned so the disc connected to the bar that is connected to the motor is lined up so the disc passes under the horizontal bars disc that is at end of horizontal bar. The high permeability material keeps the magnetic fields in disc from hitting into the disc that spins or pushes the horizontal bar until just the right moment when the impact will cause the disc in horizontal bar to spin which will perpetuate the piston motion in the horizontal bar. More horizontal bars of the same design are built and positioned around the motor segment in a circular horizontal fashion all the way around. The bar connected to the motor can be increased in length to desired height (As much as motor can handle) and more of the exact same setup is repeated higher and higher up, maximizing the over unity potential of the construct to ridiculous proportions. :D Current from the horizontal bars is diverted to power the motor as needed and all other current is diverted to power my game console or the world. :) Interesting variation to this design would be to connect the spinning discs on far ends of horizontal bar to main bar that magnets are connected to so the magnets spin instead of getting pistoned back and forth. So long as moving magnetic fields are perpendicular to copper wires it should work. By AMA

author: @Jkirk3279
text:
So: it’s similar to wind chimes.

They sway in the wind.

Put a coil inside the tubes and a magnet, so every time the tube swings to and fro, the magnet moves across the coil.

The energy of the wind pushes electrons through the coils.

A diode insures the electrons only flow in one direction.

You combine all the positive wires together.

If you had a hundred chimes tuned to different wavelengths, you’d have fairly steady trickle current.

author: @r-platt
text:
It seems that your artificial muscle is just a spring.  The whole idea is to tune the spring to respond to certain frequencies.  Spring weight (mass) and tension will determine this frequency.  The spring will react greatest to any multiple or divisor of this frequency.

Placing the magnets in the middle of the spring will result in greater movement and impart more energy to the coil.
Also, this only works when the energy is parallel to the pipe.

If you place 3 pipes pointing up/down, left/right, and forward/backward you can capture movement from any direction.  The magnitude of the 3 generators even shows a vector pointing at the source of the vibration.  I should think an FFT analysis on each output would show multiple frequencies and allow direction finding the direction for each frequency simultaneously.

text:
If this device flows 200mA at one sharp pulse, that pulse  would need t would need to be about 5 times bigger to flow one amp.
Then the pulsing would need to be continuous. Remember that power is work over time.
Most US homes are set up to draw up to 200 amps at one time. So you would need an array about 1000 times bigger than in this video under constant vibration to ensure powering such a home. 
I'm not sure what zoning you could find which might allow some way to build that. 
I'm also not coming up with realistic ideas of what constant source of vibration would do the job.
So, I agree development would be a fine hobby, without expecting anyone's going to be doing much physical work with a home array. Stupid math.

Came up with some improvements (about halfway through video):

    1. The tube uses air entering through the orifice on the side to shake a diaphragm that generates electricity, and it can vibrate up and down along the length axis of the tube. Using air, it would be more effective and powerful to use a focusing horn to make the pressure vibrations larger.

    2. Using different diameters, weights, and elasticity of diaphragms you can absorb a certain frequency of oscillation that is left out by the standard design, including using this to absorb bridge vibrations like a dampener or energy recovery from traffic.

    3. This is not much different to how an unpowered microphone works.

    4. Using materials that have a higher stiffness, or young's modulus, allows energy to transfer into the diaphragm more efficiencly without various substructures flexing, as well as reducing the overall weight.

    5. There is not much benefit to using artificial plastic muscles versus a standard spring or pneumatic, unless the flexural energy lost to heat is lower than other materials and methods.*

author: @danratsnapnames
text: i applaud your enthusiasm.  being a bit of a electronic engineer, i can understand the mechanics of this.. however, i have to caution you on a number of things..  ma's alone are not going to do anything useful.   take watts law for instance.  if you have 250 MA's at 1v,  then your only doing 250 millionths of a watt.  which is near nothing.  to get to 1 watt at 1 volt, you would have to have 4 of those.. now mind you at 1 watt, you cant do much either.  because you still have to step it up to a useful voltage.   so, consider that a 2amp 5v usb charger to charge 1 cell phone,  would take about 10 watts..   with your gizmo, you would need upwards of 40 of these units to charge 1 cell phone.   honestly,  you would likely have much better results using a real spring, instead of a makeshift one,  and you would have likely better results from an external mechanical form of energy moving it, but with that said, there are many other useful designs for utilizing different forms of energy..  windmill for one can generate upwards of 1,000 watts with nothing more than a small engine alternator mounted on a 4x4 at 6 feet tall using small 1x6's as prop blades,  which would in turn easily charge 10 cell phones with 1 unit.  yes i agree that many small units can add up..  BUT,  only if they do not have a deteriorating affect.    these would have a deteriorating affect because the more you place together they would work against each other, because one would charge the coil of another which in turn resists the movement of the other and prevents it from generating more.

text: “A lot of little things” compare that to the leaves on a tree - they all contribute a little to the energy and hence growth of the tree (through photosynthesis, Kreb’s cycle, electron chain transfer etc). author: @alanclarke4646
text: Could a vibration electricity generator be adapted to be powered by rainfall? There is an awful lot of potential energy in clouds. For instance, a cloud slightly more than 12 metres long and same wide and high ( assuming cube- shaped clouds), would condense to 1 cubic metre of water, which has a mass of 1000kg. If this cloud is 1000 metres above ground level, then the potential energy ( given by E= mgh, where g = acceleration due to gravity) comes out at 9.81 Mega Joules. This energy is converted to kinetic energy as the rain falls, and is then dissipated as noise and heat when the rain hits the ground. Surely there must be some way to recover some of the "rainfall energy"?

author: @Baigle1
text:
Came up with some improvements (about halfway through video):

    1. The tube uses air entering through the orifice on the side to shake a diaphragm that generates electricity, and it can vibrate up and down along the length axis of the tube. Using air, it would be more effective and powerful to use a focusing horn to make the pressure vibrations larger.

    2. Using different diameters, weights, and elasticity of diaphragms you can adsorb a certain frequency of oscillation that is left out by the standard design, including using this to adsorb bridge vibrations like a dampener or energy recovery from traffic.

    3. This is not much different to how an unpowered microphone works.

    4. Using materials that have a higher stiffness, or young's modulus, allows energy to transfer into the diaphragm more efficiencly without various substructures flexing, as well as reducing the overall weight.

    5. There is not much benefit to using artificial plastic muscles versus a standard spring or pneumatic, unless the flexural energy lost to heat is lower than other materials and methods.

author: @kwatt-engineer796
text: A few thoughts , if you take energy from  a tuned system you will  quickly damp the vibrations that provide the energy input to the coil.  So the output will be dependent on the energy input to the tuned system.  energy extracted from the system will have to be reduced sufficiently so that the system still resonates enough to get a useful amplitude of motion between the coil and magnet.   A next consideration will be how much energy is lost to internal friction in the tension element.   A parallel consideration might be the rising air effect in a vertical stack due to solar heating.  I can't comment on theamount of  energy input due to solar heating of air in a stack compared to energy derived from vibrations.  My guess is that energy input due to solar heating would far exceed energy input from from random vibrations.  I have observed the velocity of wind rushing up a tall stack. the effect could be amplified by painting the stack flat black   A wind generator placed at the base of such a stack might generate useful amounts of energy.The overarching question is can you harvest enough energy to warrant the capital expense?

replies.author: @kwatt-engineer796
replies.text: @icarusunited I think the answer is contained in your post. The energy transfer is via the change in  potential energy of the water as it moves from a higher elevation to a lower one via the water wheel.  Resonant frequency is not a part of the energy collected from the system.   Think of resonant energy like that in a bell. The bell will cease ringing a soon as you put your hand on it. Energy in the ringing bell is contained in the moving momentum of the mass in the bell. as soon as you touch it the energy of the momentum is transferred to your hand. There is no reason to try to put energy back in the bell in order to harvest energy to do useful work.  Hang in there, critical thinking and asking questions one of the most valuable skills you can cultivate. Chase the facts, question everything, especially yourself.

Came up with some improvements (about halfway through video):

    1. The tube uses air entering through the orifice on the side to shake a diaphragm that generates electricity, and it can vibrate up and down along the length axis of the tube. Using air, it would be more effective and powerful to use a focusing horn to make the pressure vibrations larger.

    2. Using different diameters, weights, and elasticity of diaphragms you can absorb a certain frequency of oscillation that is left out by the standard design, including using this to absorb bridge vibrations like a dampener or energy recovery from traffic.

    3. This is not much different to how an unpowered microphone works.

    4. Using materials that have a higher stiffness, or young's modulus, allows energy to transfer into the diaphragm more efficiencly without various substructures flexing, as well as reducing the overall weight.

    5. There is not much benefit to using artificial plastic muscles versus a standard spring or pneumatic, unless the flexural energy lost to heat is lower than other materials and methods.*
Warie Porbeni
Founder and president of Radio Zeater Club radiozeaterclub@gmail.com*

author: @etmax1
text:
Biggest problem with power generation from parasitic methods such as this is how much space you need per generated watt. let's say you have a solar panel that produces 1mW per m2. You may need more than the earth's usable surface area to produce enough power. Note I have not tried to calculate at what point this level would be reached, I'm just making a point. Anyhow, that is what I see as being the problem here. Yes it works, but is it practical.

The other issue to consider is that the power has to come from somewhere, and if this was on a bridge and was taking energy out of the system from cars driving over it then the cars would end up needing to produce more energy than otherwise and so that more energy coming from some other system just to make this one work. Basically there's no perpetual motion.

So things to remember, volumetric efficiency is important and the generator has to convert energy from a source where energy would otherwise be wasted, ie. not from a car rolling along a road or similar.

text: Vibrations make this generator drunk, they work like an alcohol to a man who is trying to stay upright. So maybe we could use this self destabilization principle and find configuration of artificial muscles that would always try to regain balance. A simple, tall and wide cone would do, standing on it's tip and supported by small generators from all sides, but elastic springs intersected in such way self balancing of a cone mass will produce more and more vibrations over entire structure. Magnets inside coils on side where cone is tipping over will try to stay still, causing slight magnetic tension, supported by tipping weight of a cone. But this force would be easy disturbed by vibrations, making many elastic muscles trying to contract on opposite sides of a cone, displacing magnets inside stable set of coils in the process. Less stable, the better, long live roc'n roll!

author: @marvinw3070
text: Rob, what you've made is an over complicated spring reverberator.  The old 1940's design for a spring reverberator would be much easier to implement with modern and powerful magnets, a new version can achieve greater output than its progenitor.  The spring reverberator design could even be open-air with various lengths of springs therefore making it "tuned" to just about any length of standing wave submitted to it.  It would then be more efficient over a wider range of movements and put out more voltage and current.  The open air design would allow even subtle movements of air to move the loosely-tensioned springs, therefore putting out energy, even with gentle air currents.  In essence, the springs would always be moving. Just an idea.

author: @mceajc
text:
It's nice that you are coming up with ideas.

However, without any sort of numbers, the fact it works means very little. There is little point in installing 5000 of these at £3 each if they do not outperform a single £15000 conventional wind generator. Watts per £ are important, and generating an amp at no load is almost meaningless - I didn't see a resistor in your measurement set up, so I have no idea of how much energy you converted from your little tube tap. Could you do this, and we can get an idea of efficiency?
I do like the idea of using something like this on bridges. I think the big dampers they put on the millennium footbridge in London are just hydraulic dampers. They could either have used these to force oil through a generator, or done something akin to this idea to have a permanent magnet linear generator, to capture and damp the vibrations caused by footfall and wind.

author: @q.e.d.9112
text:
I have a cheap, very low powered, led torch which you shake to recharge. The body of the torch is translucent and you can see a tube with some sort of rubber buffer at each end. There’s a magnetised slug that slides back and forth in the tube and a wire coil around the central part of the tube so that slug passes right through the coil on each pass. Unlike most modern torches, this only throws a narrow beam of no remarkable intensity leading me to conclude that it consumes, perhaps 50mW at most.
Yet it takes 5 minutes of vigorous shaking to get something like two minutes of feeble light and anything less than quite vigorous shaking cuts it down to a minute or so. I have absolute confidence that devices like yours will generate electricity but I seriously question whether they will ever generate enough to even pay back the cost of the materials used to make them.
So Robert, how about measuring output in, say, W.h over a 24h period? Simply measuring instantaneous current and focussing only on the peak tells us only that a magnet moving in the vicinity of a coil will induce a current in said coil. That’s something we learnt in 3rd form. I will be pleasantly surprised if you can produce even 1W.h in 24 hours. If you could tune it to a resonant frequency you might be able to make more efficient use of the energy available but you still can’t break unity and I just don’t see where you’re “mining” the wind for more than mW or even just μW.
Even if you could break my prediction by a whole order of magnitude, it would still need 12,000,000 of these devices to replace a single 5MW offshore turbine.

author: @aquarionh2o132
text: As always, I love your projects.  Here is a new(?) one for you!  Ion generator.  Capturing the electrical differential between the ground and an elevated antenna.  Voltage increases at a rate of 100v per meter.  So, if you have an antenna at 10m, you would have the potential to draw down and store 1000v.  Amperage is dependant upon surface area of your collection antenna, and (you’ll love this) antenna wire covered in graphene (this far) appears to draw down the largest current (the more points, the more locations for gathering the electricity (ions).  Oh!  The only downside to this technology I know if tight now is that one either needs a good storage system for the power that can take power surges when storms come by, or one needs to go out and physically disconnect one or more antennas to decrease the amount of power being drawn down and stored.  At the moment there is an experimental farm that runs off of the power generated in using this method.  Once set up, it’s a “set it and forget it” system - if done right.  Cheers

replies.author: @ArchetypalCat
replies.text:
@scrapwomblecreatives6944 Madam or Sir, inventors who believe they have created a new thing of value and want to maintain  ownership / control of their invention, *ALWAYS* research the previous patents of similar items, and especially building-blocks thereto, at the US and EU patent offices.

I appreciate your enthusiasm very much, but it is not fair to others to merely assume your invention(s) are new or unique. Humbly intended:  there are certainly a lot of other people smarter than you or I.

The problem here specifically is that resonance and vibratory physics is older than you or I - and older too than Quantum Mechanics, which you vaguely reference. (See Hertz, Helmholz, Maxwell, just to name a few.) 

Also, the video mentions that there might be previously existing, patented devices. Well there are - and it's not dozens, it's hundreds in just the US PTO alone, including resonant tension-release devices of *many* sorts, from nano-scale vibratory EMF generators, to massive commercial-level tidal generators for producing electricity. And, yes (to answer Mr. Murry-Smith's question), this particular device does have related patents. (I don't have more time to list them, but they are easy to find using appropriate key words.) 

Also I have to say, echoing other posters this thread, we were  experimenting with vibratory wire-strings, coils, and magnets to produce micro amperages already in the 1970s. It was a high school electronics teacher who showed us how to do this - and he was 60 years old in 1972. In other words, you, Madam or Sir, are certainly not the first one to come up (or patent) such ideas or devices.

Please do us all - and especially yourself - a favor before you post: do your homework first.

Thanks
-H
(P.S. I have been employed previously as a patent researcher.)

author: @tonyiliffe8907
text: Interesting idea, I note you gave no indication of the voltage, which obviously would depend on the actual coil, and magnets, used. To make the energy useful I presume the output would have the be rectified, a shottky bridge would be needed for each unit as it would be impractical to phase lock a number of separate units. That would drop about 0.3 - 0.9v, depending on the diodes, so you could only harvest anything above that. Totally agree on the "many small" principal but the rectification drop might be a significant limiter on practicality, and might dictate a minimum size for useable output. Unless you have an idea for an AC capacitor? Nice One - All the Very Best!
